title: Destructuring 🎁
description: Quickly unpack Arrays and Objects instantly into fresh variables.

slides.title: Unpacking the Box 📦
Object Destructuring 🗄️
Array Destructuring 🚂
Try It Out 💻
slides.content: Look how much cleaner it is to destructure an object passed into a function instead of writing 'e.target' or 'user.data' a hundred times!

quizData.question: What happens if you try to Destructure a key that DOES NOT exist in the target object? (e.g., `let { password } = user;`)

options.text: It finds the closest matching property
It successfully creates the variable, but its value is purely 'undefined'
It creates the variable with value 'null'

quizData.explanation: JavaScript fails silently. It will build the 'password' variable as requested, check the object, find nothing, and assign 'undefined'.

slides.title: Renaming on the Fly! 🔄

quizData.question: Which set of syntax brackets is required to Destructure an Array into isolated variables by index position?
quizData.explanation: You ALWAYS match the bracket symbol to the data structure. Arrays use []. Objects use {}.
